title: Nervilia hookeriana
intro: Nervilia hookeriana, also known as Hooker's cadet, is a species of orchid native to tropical Asia. It has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of fever, cough, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Nervilia hookeriana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Nervilia hookeriana is often prepared as a decoction or tea, and is also used in the form of capsules or tablets, to treat a range of health conditions.
